notes: Test prompts only; assertions + benchmark runs land in a follow-up PR. Skill type is pattern (router). Routing-accuracy is the primary signal: does the entry skill correctly dispatch to the right downstream skill vs baseline doing ad-hoc routing.

evals.expected_output: Routes immediately to board-superpowers:consuming-card with card_number=12. Does NOT do any work itself.

evals.prompt: what should I work on today
evals.expected_output: Routes to board-superpowers:managing-board (daily routine). Does NOT do the briefing inline — that's downstream-skill territory.
Answers inline (informational query — explicitly allowed by SKILL.md § 'Routing discipline'). Reads from the first-time-user-guide reference. Does NOT route to any downstream skill.

evals.prompt: set up board-superpowers on this repo
evals.expected_output: Surfaces the manual setup walkthrough from the first-time-user-guide reference. Walks the user through Project creation + Status field + standard labels + .board-superpowers/config.yml.

evals.prompt: decompose this design doc into cards: <pasted spec>
evals.expected_output: Routes to board-superpowers:managing-board → intake routine (which then chooses whether to pass through superpowers:brainstorming for sharpening before the architect hand-decomposes into cards). Does NOT attempt to decompose itself.

evals.prompt: fix this React bug in src/auth/login.tsx
evals.expected_output: Recognizes off-topic — does NOT capture the message. Lets the agent respond normally as without the plugin. The plugin's presence shouldn't make the agent refuse off-topic work.
